text:
INDICATION:  History of rheumatoid arthritis on Rituximab, presenting with
abdominal pain, nausea, and vomiting.  Found to have a large pelvic cyst. 
Please evaluate.

TECHNIQUE:  T1- and T2-weighted multiplanar images of the abdomen and pelvis
were acquired in a 1.5 T magnet.  Intravenous contrast: 7 mL Gadavist.

COMPARISON:  CT of the abdomen and pelvis from ___, obtained at
an outside hospital.  Pelvic ultrasound from ___.

FINDINGS: 

There is a 17.4 cm (transverse) x 3.1 cm (anterior-posterior) x 23.3 cm
(cranial caudal) cystic lesion located superior to the bladder, extending
upward in the mid lower abdomen, and into the left upper quadrant, with its
most superior point at the level of the upper kidney.  The cyst itself appears
simple.  It is hyperintense on the T2 weighted images, hypointense on the T1
weighted images, and there is no nodularity or septation.  The wall is thin
and has non-measurable, though perceptible, enhancement.

It is difficult to assess from which organ this is arising, though the left
ovary is immediately adjacent to the left lateral wall of the cystic lesion
(7, 36), suggesting it is likely arising from the ovary.  Alternatively, it
may just be so close due to mass effect.  The left ovary itself otherwise
appears to be within normal limits with several follicles.

The right ovary is normal.  This is separate from the cystic lesion.  There
are multiple follicles.

The uterus is normal measuring 5.9 x 2.7 x 4.8 cm.  There are no fibroids. 
The enodmetrium measures 4 mm, which is normal in a patient of this age.  An
IUD is noted to be in satisfactory position.  The cervix and vaginal canal are
within normal limits.

There is a small amount of free fluid in the pelvis, as well as in the
bilateral pericolic gutters.

The bladder is unremarkable.  There is no pelvic or inguinal lymphadenopathy.

The imaged portions of the liver, spleen, and kidneys are normal.  The
abdominal and pelvic vasculature is normal in caliber without evidence of an
aneurysm or significant atherosclerotic plaque.  The pelvic veins are patent
without evidence of a thrombus.

The rectum is within normal limits.  Evaluation the bowel is limited due to
bowel motion.  It appears grossly normal.  There is no evidence of
obstruction.  Of note, the large cystic lesion has mass effect on multiple
loops of small bowel.

There are no concerning osseous lesions.  There is mild anterolisthesis and
disc degeneration at L5-S1 with a small disc bulge.  Note, this exam is not
optimized for evaluation of the spine.  The soft tissues are unremarkable.

IMPRESSION: 

Large simple appearing cystic lesions which appears to be arising from the
pelvis extending into the abdomen, likely from the left ovary.  Its
characteristics are most consistent with an ovarian serous cystadenoma.  Given
the lack of complex features, a serous cystadenocarcinoma is thought to be
less likely.  The other differential consideration is a benign mesenteric
cyst.

creatinine, evaluate transplanted kidney

the cortex is of normal thickness and echogenicity, pyramids are normal, there
is no urothelial thickening, and renal sinus fat is normal.  There is no
hydronephrosis and no perinephric fluid collection.  Minimal central renal
pelvic fullness is similar to the prior study.

The resistive index of intrarenal arteries ranges from 0.7 to 0.82, mildly
elevated and unchanged.  The main renal artery shows a normal waveform, with
prompt systolic upstroke and continuous antegrade diastolic flow, with peak
systolic velocity of 85.8. Vascularity is symmetric throughout transplant. The
transplant renal vein is patent and shows normal waveform.

In the superficial subcutaneous tissues of the right lower quadrant underlying
the transplantation scar is a small loculated fluid collection that measures
up to 2.1 x 1.1 cm (cc by AP).

1. Similar mildly elevated intrarenal resistive indices.  Persistent mild
fullness of the right renal collecting system/ renal pelvis without frank
hydronephrosis.
2. Small focus of fluid measuring 2.1 x 1.1 cm underlying the transplantation
scar may represent a postoperative seroma.

EXAMINATION:  CTA CHEST WITH CONTRAST

INDICATION:  ___ year old woman with recent treatment for influenza and
presumed secondary bacterial pneumonia, now under evaluation for
nausea/vomiting and (?) incidental findings of wedge-shaped peripheral
consolidations in RML/LLL.  D-dimer elevated.  Assess for recent PE.// Assess
for pulmonary embolism

TECHNIQUE:  Axial multidetector CT images were obtained through the thorax
after the uneventful administration of intravenous contrast.  Reformatted
coronal, sagittal, thin slice axial images, and oblique maximal intensity
projection images were submitted to PACS and reviewed.

DOSE:  Acquisition sequence:
   1) Stationary Acquisition 2.5 s, 0.5 cm; CTDIvol = 24.1 mGy (Body) DLP =
12.0 mGy-cm.
   2) Spiral Acquisition 3.9 s, 30.3 cm; CTDIvol = 26.9 mGy (Body) DLP = 815.8
mGy-cm.
 Total DLP (Body) = 828 mGy-cm.

COMPARISON:  None

FINDINGS: 

HEART AND VASCULATURE: Pulmonary vasculature is well opacified to the
subsegmental level without filling defect to indicate a pulmonary embolus.
Main pulmonary artery is enlarged to 3.9 cm suggesting pulmonary hypertension.
The thoracic aorta is normal in caliber without evidence of dissection or
intramural hematoma.  The heart, pericardium, and great vessels are within
normal limits. No pericardial effusion is seen.

AXILLA, HILA, AND MEDIASTINUM: No axillary, mediastinal, or hilar
lymphadenopathy is present.  No mediastinal mass.

PLEURAL SPACES: No pleural effusion or pneumothorax.

LUNGS/AIRWAYS: There peripheral regions of consolidation in the left lower
lobe, the right middle lobe as well as the right lower lobe though less
confluent.  Calcified granuloma is noted in the right upper lobe. 
Noncalcified 3 mm nodule is also noted in the right upper lobe (3:93).  The
airways are patent to the level of the segmental bronchi bilaterally.

BASE OF NECK: Visualized portions of the base of the neck show no abnormality.

ABDOMEN:  Included portion of the upper abdomen is unremarkable.

BONES: No suspicious osseous abnormality is seen.? There is no acute fracture.
Old healed left anterolateral rib fractures are noted.


IMPRESSION:


1. No pulmonary embolism or aortic abnormality.
2. Multifocal, peripheral areas of consolidation most worrisome for infection.
Followup after treatment is suggested which can be done by plain film.
3. A 3 mm right upper lobe pulmonary nodule for which no additional follow-up
is necessary unless patient has risk factors in which case optional one year
followup chest CT can be performed.
4. Pulmonary artery enlargement suggesting pulmonary hypertension.

RECOMMENDATION(S):  Follow-up chest imaging is suggested after treatment to
document resolution, can be performed by chest x-ray.  Optional one year
followup chest CT can be performed if patient has risk factors.

NON-CONTRAST HEAD CT SCAN.

HISTORY:  ___ man status post mitral valve replacement, does not move
left side.  Rule out CVA.

TECHNIQUE:  Non-contrast head CT scan.

COMPARISON STUDY:  CT scan from ___.

FINDINGS:  There is no sign for the presence of an intracranial hemorrhage.
There is a question of a 1cm area of low density seen within the region of the
right uncus, which did not appear to be present on the prior CT scan.  If
real, this finding could represent an area of developing infarction.  No other
definite interval changes are appreciated.  There is no hydrocephalus or shift
of normally midline structures.  The surrounding osseous and soft tissue
structures do not display additional abnormalities other than moderate mucosal
thickening within the sphenoid sinus, and complete opacification of the right
maxillary sinus.  The maxillary abnormality was present on the prior study but
the sphenoid sinus pathology is new.  These findings could suggest an
inflammatory process, which may have been related to recent intubation or the
mitral valve surgery.

CONCLUSION:  Possible new focus of diminished density within the right uncus,
which could indicate an area of developing infarction.  As we discussed by
telephone today, a prompt followup MR study is suggested, if clinically
feasible, to more sensitively assess for acute brain ischemia.

ADDENDUM:  There is moderate mucosal thickening within the mastoid sinuses as
well with a possible fluid component on the left.  These sinus abnormalities
again could reflect an inflammatory process related to recent intubation.

piercing site.  The patient had a history of infection and was on antibiotics
in early ___ and now fever which subsided now recently noticed some discharge
without any fever.

GE DIGITAL BILATERAL DIAGNOSTIC MAMMOGRAM WITH ICAD.

COMPARISON:  None, baseline study.

The breasts show scattered fibroglandular densities.  Bilateral nipple rings
noted.  There are no discrete masses, no spiculated masses, suspicious
clustered microcalcifications, or areas of architectural distortion. Bilateral
benign skin calcifications and punctate coarse calcifications noted.

A targeted ultrasound of the left retroareolar region was performed.  There is
some mild skin thickening; however, there is no abscess.  There are no dilated
ducts.  There are no solid or cystic masses in the retroareolar region
scanned.  As per the patient, she recently only noticed some discharge and has
no fever.

IMPRESSION:  No evidence of malignancy.

Final disposition of the discharge should depend on clinical evaluation.

BI-RADS 2 -- benign findings.

text:
HISTORY:  Male trauma patient.

STUDY:  PORTABLE SUPINE AP CHEST RADIOGRAPH.

COMPARISON:  Torso CT from outside hospital from the same day.

FINDINGS:  The patient is situated on trauma board, limiting assessment of
fine detail.  Within that limitation, the cardiomediastinal contours are
likely exaggerated by AP projection with prominence of the right upper
mediastinal contour; however comparison with prior torso CT shows no hematoma
or atelectasis in this region.  The lung volumes are low, exaggerating the
bronchovascular structures, but underlying atelectasis and/or contusion may
also be considered.  There is no large pleural effusion or pneumothorax.  A
comminuted right mid clavicular fracture is demonstrated.  Minimally displaced
rib fractures are seen in the lateral aspect of the right mid thorax, and
better assessed on outside hospital torso CT.

IMPRESSION:
1.  Prominent right mediastinal contour but no mediastinal hematoma on prior
CT; nonspecific lung opacities likely represent atelectasis although contusion
cannot be excluded.
2.  Comminuted right mid clavicular fracture and multiple minimally displaced
lateral right rib fractures.  Correlate with torso CT from the same day.

text:
ULTRASOUND-GUIDED TARGETED LIVER BIOPSY

INDICATION:  New liver lesions, concerning for recurrence of her peritoneal
cancer 

PHYSICIANS:  Dr ___, Dr ___ 

___:  

The procedure, risks, benefits and alternatives were discussed with the
patient and written informed consent was obtained.  A preprocedure timeout was
performed discussing the planned procedure, confirming the patient's identity
with three identifiers, and reviewing a checklist per ___ protocol.

Under ultrasound guidance, an entrance site was selected and the skin was
prepped and draped in the usual sterile fashion.  8 mL of 1% lidocaine
buffered with sodium bicarbonate were instilled for local anesthesia.

An 18-gauge biopsy needle was advanced into the right hepatic lobe under
ultrasound guidance via a right lateral intercostal approach and 1 core was 
obtained.

On-site cytology determined the specimen to be adequate. 

Moderate sedation was provided by administering divided doses of Versed and
fentanyl throughout the total intra-service time of 20 minutes during which
the patient's hemodynamic parameters were continuously monitored by radiology
nursing personnel.

The patient tolerated the procedure well with no immediate complication.
Estimated blood loss was less than 5 mL.

Dr. ___ attending radiologist, was present throughout the entire
procedure.  Post-procedure instructions were written in the ___ medical
record.

IMPRESSION:

Successful ultrasound-guided targeted liver biopsy. No complications. 
Pathology pending.

3.  Interval worsening of tricompartmental degenerative changes and chondrosis

compartments.  Cortical irregularity within the proximal medial tibial
plateau, with bone marrow edema may represent possible subchondral micro

loss.

HISTORY:  Pneumonia.

Three radiographs of the chest demonstrate a normal cardiomediastinal contour.
Lungs are clear.  No effusion.  Trachea is midline.  No pneumothorax.  There
is sclerosis involving the right humeral head.  Diagnostic considerations
include osteoarthritis, although avascular necrosis is not excluded.  A left
humeral fracture fixation plate and screws is seen at the edge of the imaged
field of view on the lateral projection, incompletely assessed.

IMPRESSION:
No acute cardiopulmonary disease.

Right humeral head sclerosis.  Diagnostic considerations include
osteoarthritis.  Avascular necrosis of the right humeral head is not excluded.
If clinically indicated, this finding might be better assessed with routine
right shoulder radiographs.

INDICATION:  ___ man with Meckel's cave trigeminal schwannoma, please
rule out progression or new lesions.

TECHNIQUE:
Axial FLAIR, axial DTI, sagittal T1 pre, diffusion-weighted imaging, axial,
coronal, and sagittal MP-RAGE sequences were acquired.

COMPARISON:  MRI of the head from ___.

FINDINGS:

There is an unchanged about 14 x 9 mm lesion in the left Meckel's cave with
heterogeneous enhancement, which is FLAIR isointense, with fast diffusion and
abutting the left cavernous internal carotid artery without. There is no
associated edema or mass effect on the left temporal lobe.  The left cavernous
ICA demonstrates normal flow.  The lesion slightly deviates the trigeminal
nerve fibers to the left, unchanged from the prior exam.

There is no evidence of new enhancing intracranial lesions.  The ventricles
and sulci are normal in size and configuration.  There is no infarction and no
intracranial hemorrhage.  The paranasal sinuses and mastoids are clear.  FLAIR
images show normal flow voids in the carotid arteries bilaterally.

IMPRESSION:
Stable left Meckel's cave lesion, imaging characteristics are most suggestive
of a nerve sheath tumor, likely a schwannoma with mild mass effect on the left
trigeminal nerve and the left cavernous ICA, a thrombosed aneurysm is not
completely excluded.

RIGHT ELBOW AND RIGHT WRIST, SIX VIEWS

INDICATION:  Status post distal radius fracture.  Evaluate.

FINDINGS:

Right elbow, comparison to ___.  Extensive postoperative changes of the
right elbow are seen status post ORIF of the comminuted fracture of the distal
right humerus and fixation of the olecranon and proximal ulna.  No hardware-
related complication or change in alignment is noted.  No acute fracture is
seen.  Soft tissues are otherwise unremarkable.

Right wrist, comparison to ___.  The patient is status post ORIF of the
distal right radius with a volar plate and multiple screws.  Severely
comminuted intra-articular fracture of the distal right radius is again seen.
There is chondrocalcinosis.  Displaced fracture of the ulnar styloid is again
noted.  There is also mild degenerative change of the first CMC joint and
osteopenia.

IMPRESSION:

FINDINGS:

CT OF THE CHEST WITH IV CONTRAST:  An 11 x 9 mm lung nodule is noted in the

identified.

Target lesion #1, nodule anterior to the thoracic aorta, is barely visible on
today's study and cannot be measured (2:25).

Target lesion #2, right pleural nodule, is no longer present.

Target lesion #3, right lower lobe pulmonary nodule, is also barely visible
and unchanged since the prior study (2:47).

Target lesion #4, right posterior nodule abutting the diaphragm, measures 6 x
5 mm (2:54) and is stable in size.

There is no mediastinal, axillary, or hilar lymphadenopathy.  The largest
lymph node seen in the mediastinum is located to the left of the trachea
(2:24) and measures approximately 5 mm, unchanged.  The heart size is normal.

CT OF THE ABDOMEN WITH IV AND ORAL CONTRAST:  The spleen, liver, gallbladder,
pancreas, stomach, left adrenal gland, and intra-abdominal loops of bowel are
within normal limits.  Small hypoattenuating lesions noted in the upper pole
of the right kidney are unchanged.  The left kidney has a hypoattenuating
lesion in the interpolar region, consistent with a cyst, stable.  The left
adrenal gland contains an 8 x 8 nodule, also stable.

Superior to the upper pole of the left kidney is a soft tissue density
measuring 25 x 25 mm.  This has markedly increased in size since the previous
study when it measured 6 x 9 mm.  Another soft tissue density located slightly
anterolateral to the left crux of the diaphragm (2:61) has remained stable in
size.  There is no retroperitoneal or mesenteric lymphadenopathy.  No free air
or free fluid is seen.

CT OF THE PELVIS WITH IV AND ORAL CONTRAST:  The rectum, prostate, and bladder
are within normal limits.  There is extensive diverticulosis of the sigmoid
colon without diverticulitis.  No pelvic or inguinal lymphadenopathy is seen.
There is no free fluid.

BONE WINDOWS:  No suspicious osseous lesions are identified.  However, there
are mild anterior wedge compression deformities of T11 through L1, unchanged.
Soft tissue windows demonstrate a tiny soft tissue density in the subcutaneous
fat of the left chest wall (2:27), which measures up to 2 mm and is unchanged
when compared to multiple previous studies.

IMPRESSION:
1.  Interval increase in size of right upper lobe lung nodule.  New pulmonary
nodule noted at the right lung base.
2.  Marked increase in size of soft tissue lesion superior to the upper pole
of the left kidney.
3.  Target lesions either no longer seen or stable in size.

Findings were discussed with ___, RN, shortly after review on ___.

HISTORY:  Left-sided pelvic pain x6 months.

COMPARISON:  None.

FINDINGS:  Transabdominal and transvaginal ultrasound examinations were
performed.  The latter for more detailed evaluation of endometrium and adnexa.
The uterus measures 5.1x2.6x3.2 cm. The endometrium measures 2 mm.  There is
an echogenic lesion in the lower uterus measuring 0.7 x 0.5 x 1.1 cm,
appearance consistent with polyp.  There is a small amount of fluid seen
within the endometrial canal at the fundus likely related to a degree of
obstruction related to polyp.  There is a cyst in the right ovary measuring
1.2 x 0.7 x 1.0 cm.  This contains internal echoes which may be related to
debris or artifact.  The left ovary is normal. There is no free fluid.

IMPRESSION:
1.  Echogenic polyp in the lower uterus with a trace amount of fluid seen in
the endometrium and fundus likely related to partial obstruction.

2.  Right ovarian cyst measuring up to 1.2 cm containing some internal echoes
which are felt to be related to debris or artifact.  A six-month followup is
recommended.

text:
EXAMINATION:  CT HEAD WITHOUT CONTRAST

INDICATION:  ___ male with left leg numbness.

TECHNIQUE:  Contiguous axial MDCT images were obtained through the brain
without the administration of IV contrast.  Coronal, sagittal and thin bone
slice reformats were generated.

DOSE:  DLP: 892 mGy-cm
CTDI: 54 mGy

COMPARISON:  None available.

FINDINGS: 

In the left posterior frontal/parietal lobe, abutting the inner table of the
posterior left parietal bone, there is a 2.7 cm hyperdense mass exerting mass
effect on the adjacent sulci but without significant surrounding of edema or
mass effect upon the ventricles or midline structures.  The mass appears
extraaxial.

Otherwise there is no hemorrhage or large vascular territorial infarction. The
ventricles and sulci are normal in size and configuration.  There is
preservation of grey-white matter differentiation in the non affected portions
of the brain and the basal cisterns are patent.

No fracture is identified. The paranasal sinuses, mastoid air cell and middle
ear cavities are clear.

IMPRESSION: 

Hyperdense mass without clear adjacent edema in the posterior left frontal/
parietal lobe abutting the inner table of the posterior left parietal bone is
likely extraaxial, possibly a meningioma. Contrast enhanced MRI should provide
further characterization.

text:
EXAMINATION:  CTA ABD AND PELVIS

INDICATION:  ___ year old woman with locally advanced pancreatic cancer.  //
locally advanced pancreatic cancer.  extent of disease.

TECHNIQUE:  Abdomen and pelvis CTA: Non-contrast and multiphasic post-contrast
images were acquired through the abdomen and pelvis.
Oral contrast was not administered.
MIP reconstructions were performed on independent workstation and reviewed on
PACS.

DOSE:  Acquisition sequence:
   1) Spiral Acquisition 3.5 s, 27.6 cm; CTDIvol = 20.3 mGy (Body) DLP = 559.7
mGy-cm.
   2) Spiral Acquisition 8.3 s, 65.3 cm; CTDIvol = 21.7 mGy (Body) DLP =
1,414.2 mGy-cm.
 Total DLP (Body) = 1,974 mGy-cm.

COMPARISON:  CT abdomen and pelvis from ___.

FINDINGS: 

VASCULAR:

There is no abdominal aortic aneurysm.  There is moderate calcium burden in
the abdominal aorta and great abdominal arteries.

LOWER CHEST: Please refer to the separate report of CT chest performed on the
same day for description of the thoracic findings.

ABDOMEN:

HEPATOBILIARY: There is mild pneumobilia with mild intrahepatic biliary ductal
dilatation.  Scattered regions of enhancement throughout the right lobe are
noted.  A CBD stent is in place.  The gallbladder is within normal limits,
without stones or gallbladder wall thickening.

PANCREAS: Within the uncinate process there is a hypoenhancing mass which has
overall decreased in size compared to prior measures up to approximately 2.4
times 1.5 cm, previously measuring up to 2.4 x 2.2 cm.  The mass abuts the
posterior aspect of the SMA and SMV with less than 180 decreased contact. 
Remainder the vascular structures are intact down uninvolved.  The mass
contacts the third portion of the duodenum.

SPLEEN: The spleen shows normal size and attenuation throughout, without
evidence of focal lesions.

ADRENALS: The right and left adrenal glands are normal in size and shape.

URINARY: The kidneys are of normal and symmetric size with normal nephrogram.
There is no evidence of stones, focal renal lesions, or hydronephrosis. There
are no urothelial lesions in the kidneys or ureters. There is no perinephric
abnormality.

GASTROINTESTINAL: Small bowel loops demonstrate normal caliber, wall thickness
and enhancement throughout. Colon and rectum are within normal limits. 
Appendix contains air, has normal caliber without evidence of fat stranding. 
There is no evidence of mesenteric lymphadenopathy.

RETROPERITONEUM: Multiple prominent peripancreatic and portal caval lymph
nodes are similar to prior.

PELVIS: The urinary bladder and distal ureters are unremarkable.  There is no
evidence of pelvic or inguinal lymphadenopathy.  There is no free fluid in the
pelvis.

REPRODUCTIVE ORGANS: The visualized reproductive organs are unremarkable.

BONES: There is no evidence of worrisome osseous lesions or acute fracture. 
Severe multilevel degenerative changes of the spine are again noted.

SOFT TISSUES: The abdominal and pelvic wall is within normal limits.

IMPRESSION:


1. Interval decrease in size of mass in the uncinate process with continued
abutment of the SMA and SMV.  Mass continues to abut the third portion of the
duodenum.  Multiple prominent though nonenlarged peripancreatic and portal
caval lymph nodes are stable.
2. Scattered foci of enhancement within the right lobe of the liver are likely
perfusional, however cholangitis can have a similar imaging appearance and
clinical correlation is recommended.
3. Please see separate chest CT report for chest findings.

text:
CERVICAL SPINE MRI WITH AND WITHOUT CONTRAST, ___

INDICATION:  ___ woman with intravenous drug use and recurrent MRSA
abscesses.  History of epidural abscess debridement and fusion of C4 through
C6 in ___.  Now with another cervical epidural abscess.  Evaluate for
interval change.

COMPARISON:  ___.

TECHNIQUE:  Sagittal T1-weighted, T2-weighted, and STIR images of the cervical
spine, with axial gradient echo and T2-weighted images.  Following intravenous
gadolinium administration, sagittal and axial T1-weighted images of the
cervical spine were obtained.

FINDINGS:  Again seen are edema and contrast enhancement within the C6 and C7
vertebral bodies, as well as within the intervening disc, consistent with
discitis and osteomyelitis.  There is suggestion of similar but less extensive
signal abnormalities in the C4 and C5 vertebral bodies, both on the current
and prior studies.  However, evaluation of bone marrow signal at C4 and C5 is
limited due to hardware-related artifacts.  Of note, the patient is status
post anterior discectomies and instrumented anterior fusion at C4-5 and C5-6,
but the hardware is not evaluated by MRI.  There is a right paracentral
epidural collection spanning from C5-6 through C7-T1, similar in size and
configuration to the prior exam, with unchanged moderate compression of the
right ventral cord.  No definite cord edema is seen.  The previously noted
non-enhancing area within the epidural collection has decreased in size,
indicating decreased epidural abscess and progression to more homogeneous
epidural phlegmon.  There is also a pre-vertebral rim-enhancing fluid
collection from C4 through C7, which extends into the right lateral soft
tissues towards the carotid sheath.  The craniocaudad extent of the collection
has decreased from 6 to 4 cm, and the maximal axial cross section of the
collection has also slightly decreased.

The imaged portion of the posterior fossa is unremarkable.  Previously noted
nasopharyngeal secretions have resolved.

IMPRESSION:

1.  Unchanged appearance of discitis and osteomyelitis at C6-7.  Bone marrow
edema and contrast enhancement may also be present within the C4 and C5
vertebral bodies, on both the current and prior studies, but evaluation is
limited by hardware-related artifacts.

2.  The right paracentral epidural collection at C5-6 through C7-T1 is stable
in size, but demonstrates a decreased abscess component with progression to a
more homogeneous phlegmon.

3.  The pre-vertebral fluid collection, which extends towards the right
carotid sheath, has decreased in size.

INDICATION:  ___ male with difficulty swallowing, here to evaluate for
aspiration.

COMPARISON:  No prior studies available.

TECHNIQUE:  Oropharyngeal swallowing videofluoroscopy was performed in
conjunction with the speech and swallow division.  Multiple consistencies of
barium were administered.

FINDINGS:  Barium passes freely through the oropharynx into the esophagus
without evidence of obstruction.  There was deep penetration of nectar thick
liquids.  Moderate-to-large amount of vallecular residue was noted with
nectar-thick liquids and puree consistency.  There was gross aspiration of
vallecular residue with subsequent swallows.  Cervical anterior disc
osteophyte complexes are noted without significant narrowing of the upper
esophagus.  For further details, please refer to the speech and swallow
division note in the OMR.

IMPRESSION:
1.  Deep penetration of nectar-thick liquids.
2.  Moderate-to-large vallecular residue with nectar-thick and puree
consistencies.
3.  Gross aspiration of vallecular residue on subsequent swallowing.

INDICATION:  Dysphagia.

effusion or pneumothorax. Vague opacity projecting over region of otherwise
unchanged rib fractures suggests mild associated pleural thickening at that
site. Otherwise, lung fields appear clear.

Feeding tube terminating in the gastric antrum.

text:
EXAMINATION:  Right internal carotid artery angiogram.

Left internal carotid artery angiogram.

Right vertebral artery angiogram.

Right common femoral artery angiogram.

INDICATION:  ___ year old man with SAH w IVH, possible acom aneurysm per CTA.//
___ year old man with SAH w IVH, possible acom aneurysm per CTA.
ANESTHESIA:  General endotracheal anesthesia was maintained by separate
anesthesia provider throughout the entirety of the case.The anesthesia
provider also monitored the patient's hemodynamic and respiratory parameters.

TECHNIQUE:
Patient was brought into the angio suite, ID was confirmed via wrist band.The
patient was placed supine on fluoroscopy table and bilateral groins were
prepped and draped in the usual sterile manner. Time-out procedure was
performed per institutional guidelines. The location of the right mid femoral
head was located using anatomic and radiographic landmarks.  10 +10 cc of
subcutaneous lidocaine was infused into the tissue.  Micropuncture kit was
used to gain access to the right femoral artery, serial dilation was
undertaken until a long 6 ___ groin sheath connected to a continuous
heparinized saline flush could be inserted. ___ catheter was
connected to the power injector and also to a continuous heparinized saline
flush. This was advanced over the 0.038 glidewire brought up the aorta used to
select the right internal carotid artery. AP, oblique and lateral views of the
anterior cerebral circulation were obtained.  Subsequently, 3D rotational
images were performed requiring post processing on an independent workstation
under concurrent physician supervision and used in the interpretation and
reporting of the procedure.
The catheter was then pulled back in the aorta and the right subclavian artery
was selected. AP road map imaging was undertaken.  Next, the right vertebral
artery was selected. AP and lateral views were taken from this vessel for the
posterior cerebral circulation.

Catheter was then pulled back in the aorta and used to select the left
internal carotid artery. AP, oblique and lateral views of the anterior
cerebral circulation were obtained.

Next the catheter was then pulled back into the aorta and used to cannulate
the right internal carotid artery.

Then, in collaboration with our colleagues anesthesia, 5000 units of heparin
were given to target ACT between 250 and 300 subsequent doses were given as
needed to achieve this target.

Under constant fluoroscopy, using an angled Glidewire exchange, the diagnostic
catheter was exchanged to Benchmark catheter, which was advanced to
satisfactory position in the internal carotid artery. The Glidewire was
removed, a new AP and lateral road maps were obtained, then SL 10 micro
catheter was advanced carefully and slowly over a synchro 2 wire until its was
positioned halfway into the aneurysm.

2.5mm/3.3cm MicrusFrame 10 Coil was used as a framing coil, which was advanced
slowly and carefully until it was fully deployed inside the aneurysm, before
final detachment an angio run was done confirming patency of the ACA complex. 
Subsequently, we continued deploying a more coils until we reached
satisfactory obliteration of the aneurysm, intermittent angio runs were
utilized between the coiling sessions.

After the deployment of the last coil, the micro catheter was pulled out, then
we obtained final AP and lateral views, which confirmed the patency of all
involved arteries and a satisfactory obliteration of the aneurysm.

The catheter was then pulled back in the aorta fully removed from the body.  A
common femoral arteriogram was performed prior to use of a closure device,
subsequently Angio-Seal was put in. At the conclusion of the procedure, there
is no evidence of thromboembolic complication and the patient was at his
neurologic baseline.


Devices inventory:
.038" 150cm Angled Glidewire
035 x 150cm ___ Wire
___ x 25cm Terumo Sheath Set
___ Berenstein ___ 100cm Cath.
038 Angled Glidewire Exchange
___ .071 95cm Benchmark Delivery Catheter
Synchro2 Standard 14 200cm Wire
Excelsior SL-10 150cm Microcatheter
2.5mm/3.3cm MicrusFrame 10 Coil LOT#___
InZone Detachment System
Target  360 Ultra 3mm/4cm Coil ___
Target  360 Ultra 2.5mm/4cm Coil ___
___ Angio Seal Evolution Closure Device ___

COMPARISON:  None.

PROCEDURE:  Coiling of an A-comm aneurysm.

FINDINGS:


Right internal carotid artery:  The distal right ICA, proximal and distal MCA
and ACA branches are well-visualized.  Robust PCOM compatible with fetal
variant.  An A-comm aneurysm was identified measuring 7.4 mm in maximum
diameter and 3.4 mm from dome to neck and a 4.1 mm at the neck.  No
cross-filling to the contralateral A2.  Post coiling, successful obliteration
of the aneurysm compatible with ___ grade 1 with one coil loop
protruding from the neck.  No flow compromise or stagnation.  Otherwise,
vessel caliber smooth and tapering. Normal arterial, capillary, and venous
phase .

Left internal carotid artery:  Distal left ICA, proximal and distal MCA and
ACA branches are well-visualized.  No cross-filling to the contralateral A2. 
Robust PCOM compatible with fetal variant.  Vessel caliber smooth and
tapering. Normal arterial, capillary, and venous phase . No vascular
abnormalities identified .

Right vertebral artery, right ___, basilar artery, bilateral AICA, bilateral
SCA and bilateral PCAs are well-visualized. The right ___ is not well
visualized as there was no cross-filling to the left vertebral artery.  No
vascular abnormalities identified, vessel caliber smooth and tapering.
Arterial, capillary, venous phases were normal .

Right common femoral artery: Well-visualized with a good caliber size for
closure device.


I, ___, participated in the procedure.  I, ___,
was present for the entirety of the procedure and supervised all critical
steps.

I, ___, have reviewed the report and agree with the fellow's
findings.

IMPRESSION: 

An A-comm aneurysm was identified measuring 7.4 mm in maximum diameter and 3.4
mm from dome to neck and a 4.1 mm at the neck.  No cross-filling to the
contralateral A2.  Post coiling, successful obliteration of the aneurysm
compatible with ___ grade 1 with one coil loop protruding from the
neck. No flow compromise or stagnation.

RECOMMENDATION(S):
1. Start on 81 mg of aspirin, management of subarachnoid hemorrhage as per
usual protocol.

text:
INDICATION:  Epigastric pain in patient with end-stage renal disease and known
portal vein thrombosis.

COMPARISON:  Liver ultrasound from ___, CT abdomen from ___, and MRI abdomen ___.

TECHNIQUE:  Multidetector CT-acquired axial images from the lung bases to the
pubic symphysis were displayed with 5-mm slice thickness.  Image acquisition
was performed after IV contrast administration.  PO contrast was not given. 
Multiplanar reformation was performed to generate sagittal and coronal image
series.

ABDOMEN:  There is minimal left basilar atelectasis as well as coronary
arterial calcifications.  The lower chest is otherwise unremarkable.

The liver has a macronodular contour, consistent with known history of
cirrhosis.  The gallbladder is absent.  No focal liver lesions are seen. 
There is thrombosis in the main portal vein, which appears chronic given its
calcified rim, which extends into the porta hepatis and undergoes cavernous
change at the origin of the occluded left portal vein (2:14, 24).  The splenic
vein and SMV appear patent.  The left portal vein is not well seen which is a
new finding from the prior ultrasound on ___, when flow was
demonstrated.  The pancreas and adrenal glands appear normal.  There is stable
splenomegaly as well as ascites.  The bilateral kidneys enhance normally and
excrete contrast symmetrically.  There is a small hiatal hernia.  The stomach
and proximal duodenum are decompressed, limiting evaluation.

There is a large but stable umbilical hernia with multiple loops of
unobstructed small bowel and mesentery along with ascitic fluid within the
hernia sac.  The abdominal loops of small and large bowel feature no wall
thickening or dilation.  There is diffuse mesenteric vessel varicosity,
unchanged from the prior studies.

There is no intra-abdominal free air.  No lymphadenopathy is identified. 
There are atherosclerotic calcifications within the abdominal aorta, but the
main branches are patent.

PELVIS:  Increased bowel wall thickening is noted of the right hemicolon when
compared to the prior cross-sectional studies (2:45).  The transverse and
descending colon are normal.  There is sigmoid diverticulosis without
diverticulitis.  There is no pelvic abscess or lymphadenopathy.  The normal
appendix is visualized.  The uterus, bladder, and adnexa appear normal.

BONE WINDOWS:  There is no acute fracture, malalignment, or lesion concerning
for malignancy.  Incidental note is made of an L5 pars defect.

IMPRESSION:
1.  Increased right colonic wall thickening, maybe due to portal colopathy but
infectious or ischemic colitis cannot be excluded. No obvious thromboembolus
in mesenteric vessels. 
2.  Chronic thrombosis of the main portal vein extending to the left portal
vein, with non-visualization of the right portal vein.  This may be due to the
timing of image acquisition or progression of portal vein thrombosis.  If
clinically indicated, consider Doppler ultrasound study of the liver.
3.  Hepatic cirrhosis with sequelae of portal hypertension including ascites,
splenomegaly, and extensive mesenteric varicosity.
4.  Large but stable umbilical hernia containing multiple loops of
non-obstructed small bowel, free fluid, and omentum/mesentery.

text:
INDICATION:  ___ male with fall from 20 feet.  

No prior examinations for comparison.

TECHNIQUE:  Helical MDCT images were acquired through the torso with
intravenous contrast and without oral contrast.  5 mm STIR, coronal, and
sagittal multiplanar reformats were generated.

FINDINGS

CHEST:  A pleural bleb is noted at the left lung apex.  The lungs are clear,
other than mild bibasilar atelectasis.  The distal airways are patent to the
subsegmental levels.  There are no pleural effusions.

Within the limits of contrast bolus timing, there is no main or lobar
pulmonary embolus.  The thoracic aorta is normal in caliber without evidence
of active extravasation, aneurysm, or dissection.  The aortic arch has a
normal three-vessel branch morphology.  The heart is normal in size, without
pericardial effusion.

Prominent right hilar node measures 8 mm.  Other axillary, mediastinal, and
hilar lymph nodes are not pathologically enlarged.

Note is made of a small sliding hiatal hernia.

ABDOMEN:  The liver enhances homogeneously, without focal lesions identified
on this single phase examination.  The gallbladder is partially collapsed. 
The pancreas is normal.  There is no intra- or extra-hepatic biliary ductal
dilatation.  The spleen is normal in size.

The adrenals are normal.  The kidneys enhance and excrete contrast promptly
and symmetrically, without masses or hydronephrosis.

The stomach is filled with ingested material and air.  The small bowel is
normal.  

PELVIS:  The appendix is surgically absent.  The colon and rectum are
unremarkable.

The bladder is normal.  Bilateral ureteral jets are visualized.  The prostate
and seminal vesicles are unremarkable.  There is no free intraperitoneal fluid
or air.

Retroperitoneal and mesenteric lymph nodes are not pathologically enlarged.

No fractures are identified.  Subcutaneous locules of gas are noted in the
left posterior hip, reflecting known subcutaneous laceration in the left lower
extremity.

IMPRESSION:  

No evidence of acute visceral injury in the chest, abdomen, or pelvis.  No
acute fracture seen.

Subcutaneous locules of gas noted in the left posterior hip, reflecting known
subcutaneous laceration in the left lower extremity.
